question_text: A mechanic uses a wrench to tighten a bolt. Which statement best explains why using a longer wrench makes the job easier?

options.label: A
options.text: A longer wrench is heavier, so it provides more downward force.

options.label: B
options.text: A longer wrench increases the distance from the pivot (bolt), which increases the moment for the same effort force.

options.label: C
options.text: A longer wrench reduces friction at the bolt.

options.label: D
options.text: A longer wrench spreads the force over a larger area.

explanation: A longer wrench works as a longer lever, and since moment = force × distance, increasing the distance from the pivot means a larger turning moment is created with the same effort force. This is why longer wrenches are better for tight bolts.
